business.headline: What's driving gold's rally? World Gold Council's Sachin Jain explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Sachin Jain, Regional CEO (India) at the World Gold Council, said the factors supporting gold prices remain strong.

Are foreign powers blocking India's energy self-reliance? Anil Agarwal answersVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said India has the resources to produce more energy domestically but policy changes and faster decision-making are needed to unlock the sector.

Could rising gas prices make your quick commerce orders costlier?Quick commerce has become the go-to option for instant groceries and food deliveries. But with LPG prices rising and supply concerns growing, the cost pressure may be building behind the scenes. Could this soon make your quick commerce orders costlier? Let's have a look.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Early indicators show a weak start for the benchmark indices. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,549.5 at around 07:57 am. This signals that the Nifty 50 is likely to open below Thursday's closing level of 23,639.15.

Live: Panic at LPG agencies in Jammu as residents rush to enquire about bookingLPG crisis live updates: Concerns over cooking gas supply are beginning to affect daily life across several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. Top private schools in Delhi have also issued warnings that lunch services could be affected if LPG supplies tighten further. Several malls in the national capital have asked restaurants to operate conservatively and manage operations with about 80% gas supply. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic. C Senthil Rajan, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, said oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day even during the crisis and that supplies remain intact. Police in several states have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and illegal trade in cylinders. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the impact across cities and industries in India.

No fuel shortage in India, supply chains stable: Hardeep Singh Puri in ParliamentSpeaking in Parliament, Union Petroleum and Natural Gas Minister Hardeep Singh Puri said India has adequate fuel stocks, and there is no cause for concern over availability.

Retail inflation rises to 3.21% in February, higher than 2.75% in JanuaryThe slight uptick in inflation was mainly driven by changes in food prices, which carry a significant weight in the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

Tech Mahindra onboarding delay keeps over 1,000 freshers waiting: ReportMany of the affected graduates are from colleges in Maharashtra. They had cleared several stages of the recruitment process earlier in 2025 but are yet to receive formal offer letters or confirmed joining dates.

Sensex ends over 820 points lower, Nifty below 23,700; Coal India gains 5%Markets remained volatile through the session, with the Nifty touching a low of 23,556.30 during the day before trimming some losses by the close. Selling was seen across several heavyweight stocks, particularly in auto, banking and capital goods sectors.

Gulf conflict raises insurance costs for Indian airlines, fares may riseIndustry sources told India Today TV that airline operators are now paying additional insurance charges for every aircraft flying into the Middle East, significantly increasing the cost of operations on these routes.

Stocking LPG cylinders during shortage? Know the legal limits and penaltiesUnder law, a domestic household is generally allowed to keep two LPG cylinders of 14.2 kg each at a time. This usually includes one cylinder connected for use and another spare cylinder kept as backup.

Rupee sinks to record low of Rs 92.36 against US dollarThe currency weakened as global markets reacted to escalating tensions in West Asia and a sharp jump in oil prices, with Brent crude climbing above $100 per barrel.

Brent crude back above $100 per barrel: Why are oil prices rising again?Brent crude has crossed $100 again. With tanker attacks and rising war tensions threatening supply routes, the big question now is how long oil prices will stay this high.

entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 adds last-minute opera-ballet act amid Timothee Chalamet row: Report Misty Copeland will perform at the 2026 Oscars shortly after responding to Timothee Chalamet's viral comments about ballet and opera. The timing has sparked widespread discussion.

Don't use Jailer, Coolie songs in promos: Makers take Chennai Super Kings to court Makers of Jailer, Jailer 2 and Coolie filed a copyright infringement suit against Chennai Super Kings for using music and dialogues from their films. The case was heard at the Madras High Court on March 13.

Will Katrina Kaif and Ranbir Kapoor reunite for Raajneeti 2? Director reacts Prakash Jha confirmed he is actively working on Raajneeti 2, years after the political drama's success. What could the sequel mean for the original cast?

BTS' OT7 era begins: ARIRANG first look out ahead of long-awaited comeback With seven days until the OT7 comeback, BTS shared the first glimpse of its upcoming album ARIRANG with a historical animation inspired by Korea's iconic folk song. Kapoor family copyrights Rishi Kapoor's name posthumously to prevent misuse The Kapoor family has taken legal steps to protect the name and legacy of late actor Rishi Kapoor. This move ensures no unauthorised commercial use of his name without family's permission.

Late Rob Reiner to be honoured at Oscars 2026, confirms Conan O'Brien The 98th Academy Awards will honour the late Rob Reiner with a powerful tribute, following his and his wife's death in December. general news.headline: 4 killed as US military aircraft crashes in Iraq, Iran-backed group claims roleA US KC-135 refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq during Operation Epic Fury, prompting rescue efforts. India and Iran: Blood Is Thicker Than BabelFrom "madar" and "matr" to "haft" and "sapta", the scores of parallels between Persian and Sanskrit are more than coincidence. They are linguistic reminders of a shared civilisation that is 4,000 years old and remains intertwined even today.

The OG India Today newsroom: Veteran editors relive the iconic cover meetingAt the India Today Conclave 2026, Editor-in-Chief Aroon Purie joined a panel of veteran journalists for a simulated editorial meeting, bringing together decades of newsroom experience to discuss possible cover story ideas. The session featured prominent media voices including Raj Chengappa, Prabhu Chawla, TN Ninan, Swapan Dasgupta, Shekhar Gupta and Kaveree Bamzai.

Live: Blast near protest rally in Tehran; Trump claims Iran about to surrenderUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran war entered day 14. New Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, issued his first statement since the conflict began on February 28. In the message released by Tehran, he warned that US bases in the region will be attacked. Conflicting reports also emerged about his condition, with some suggesting he was wounded during the initial US-Israeli strikes that killed his father, former supreme leader Ali Khamenei. Meanwhile, Iran continued launching missile and drone attacks across the Gulf region, triggering air defence responses in several countries. Sirens sounded again in Doha as regional forces intercepted incoming projectiles, while the UAE and Saudi Arabia said they shot down multiple ballistic missiles and drones. Tehran has also targeted shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil supplies. Israel, in turn, intensified strikes on Iranian-linked targets, including Basij forces in Tehran and Hezbollah-linked sites in Beirut.

President wants you right away: Scott Bessent summoned by Trump mid-interviewUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent was summoned by President Donald Trump while the former was giving a live interview at the White House. The journalist waited for nearly two hours before the chat resumed.

When India set sea ablaze, sank 100,000 tonnes of Pakistani ships with minesIran has populated the Strait of Hormuz, a key energy route, with sea mines. The 1971 War saw the most significant use of sea mines since World War Two in which India sank over 100,000 tonnes of Pakistani shipping. The marine mines helped India choke Pakistan's supply lines in the Ganga delta.

This is due to the Mosaic Defence Doctrine of Mohammad Ali Jafari. Jafari took lessons from the US invasion of Iraq in 2003, and ensured Tehran would remain resilient even if its top leadership was taken out.

When Indira Gandhi got India Today copies picked up over Nellie massacre coverThe Nellie massacre in Assam in 1983 was one of the deadliest episodes of ethnic violence that left 3,000 dead. India Today ran a cover story on the incident. However, then PM Indira Gandhi allegedly had copies of the magazine picked up from newsstands. The incident was recounted by journalist TN Ninan during the India Today Conclave 2026.
